Title: Metabolic engineering of bread wheat improves grain iron concentration and bioavailability

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Constitutive transgenic expression in wheat under maize ubiquitin 1 promoter; qRT-PCR; LC-MS quantification of nicotianamine and DMA; ICP-OES/ICP-MS elemental profiling; synchrotron X-ray fluorescence microscopy; Caco-2 ferritin bioavailability assay; field trials
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High transgene expression in roots and shoots of CE-1 seedlings
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Glasshouse and field conditions; multi-location field trials at Katanning and Merredin
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Grain, especially endosperm for Fe enrichment and crease region/white flour for Zn-associated enrichment
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves plant metal transport/nutrition; increases iron bioavailability in human intestinal cell model
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of grain Fe, Zn, nicotianamine and DMA and increases Fe bioavailability in white flour without major agronomic penalties
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Constitutive expression of rice OsNAS2 in wheat markedly increased grain nicotianamine (~15-fold in mature grain) and elevated DMA. 2. CE-OsNAS2 events showed increased grain Fe and/or Zn, with enhanced Fe localization throughout grain tissues, especially the endosperm, and altered Zn enrichment in crease-associated tissues. 3. White flour from field-grown CE-OsNAS2 lines had increased NA and DMA, and Fe bioavailability in Caco-2 cells positively correlated with NA and DMA rather than flour Fe concentration. 4. Most agronomic traits and yield were not adversely affected.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate transgene for wheat Fe biofortification and improved Fe bioavailability; useful for endosperm-targeted mineral improvement and could be combined with additional Fe-loading or low-phytate strategies
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major penalties detected for shoot area, plant height, grain number, thousand grain weight, biomass, or yield in most events/sites; discussion notes possible competition for endogenous SAM but no clear adverse phenotype observed
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TaNAS; TaNAAT; TaDMAS (examined as endogenous pathway genes, not shown to physically interact)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsNAS2 is a transporter-related metal chelator biosynthesis gene used here as the sole core engineered gene. Endosperm Fe enrichment did not co-localize with P, suggesting Fe in white flour is not primarily associated with phytic acid. White flour Zn likely derives largely from non-endosperm tissues such as the crease.
